description: BBI Model Performance Tracker — Spearman rank correlation with DG head-to-head comparison, field-size normalization, weekly improvement engine, and layer health across all events

goal_framework.ultimate_goal: Best-rated predictive golf model in the world

tier_1_baseline.label: Beat DataGolf
tier_1_baseline.description: Prove BBI adds value over a pure strokes-gained model by outperforming DG pre-tournament rankings week over week

tier_2_target.label: Elite Model
tier_2_target.description: Sustained 0.40+ across diverse courses and field sizes puts us in top-tier territory

tier_3_aspirational.label: Best in World
tier_3_aspirational.description: Maintaining 0.50+ across a full season with head-to-head DG dominance is the ultimate claim

weekly_improvement_protocol.description: After each event, Mnemosyne analyzes what worked and what didn't, then suggests 1-3 specific weight or methodology changes for the following week

baseline_spearman: dynamic — computed from DG each week
notes: v3.0: Now tracking DG Spearman head-to-head each week. Baseline is no longer a static 0.269 estimate — Mnemosyne computes DG's actual rank correlation from their pre-tournament predictions vs actual results. Goal tiers: Beat DG → 0.40 season avg → 0.50 season avg.

normalization_method.description: Fisher z-transform accounts for field-size differences. Larger fields (135) and smaller fields (85) produce different raw Spearman values for the same model quality. The z-score normalizes this so we can compare Masters (85 players) to Valero (132 players) fairly.
normalization_method.reference_note: Standard PGA Tour field is ~135 players. Masters is ~85. z-scores allow direct comparison.

events.notes: First tracked event. Fitzpatrick was a top-5 BBI pick. | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method).
Woodland win from rank #88 — deep field surprise. Model still ranked field well overall despite missing winner. | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method).
Best Spearman yet. APP layer validated (+2.19 for winner). Spaun was within BBI credible range. | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method).

events.dg_note: DG Spearman uses current global skill rankings as proxy — pre-tournament predictions unavailable post-event. DG ranks McIlroy #2 (winner), Scheffler #1 (2nd) — excellent calibration at top.
events.notes: First event below 0.40 target. Masters-specific challenge: 91-player invitational field including LIV veterans, past champions, and Augusta specialists outside normal PGA rankings. Winner McIlroy (BBI #9, ELITE tier) correctly identified. Key misses: Rose and Hatton (both T3, both unranked by BBI). Aberg (#2 BBI) dramatically over-ranked based on 2-start Augusta sample. | Recomputed 2026-05-05 with scipy.stats.spearmanr; prior values (BBI 0.3824, DG 0.6659) used inconsistent methodology.
manual catch-up run — scheduled Mnemosyne stalled Mon Apr 20 | Recomputed 2026-05-05 with consistent scipy methodology.
Zurich Classic team format — paused_team_event mode (team event — pipeline deferred)
All 70 players made cut (small field 72). Spearman 0.303 (p=0.011, significant). Top-10 overlap 4/10. Cameron Young BBI#2 wins. | Recomputed 2026-05-05 with consistent scipy methodology.
Reitan wins (BBI #43, DG #36). Low-ranked surprise winner. BBI beat DG narrowly.

events.dg_note: DG pre-tournament predictions unavailable post-event (endpoint returns 0 players for completed events). DG Spearman not computed this week.
events.notes: BBI above 0.40 target at a major with zero venue history — best major performance of season. Surprise winner Aaron Rai (BBI #63) was a near-miss — a LIV player with elite putting stats not well-captured by global skill rankings.
Backfilled by Mnemosyne 2026-06-08 — pipeline ran but postmortem was skipped in June 1 run. Henley (BBI #4) wins at -20 in a playoff over Cole. DG preds unavailable post-event.

events.event: the Memorial Tournament presented by Workday
events.notes: J.T. Poston (BBI #51) wins playoff over Gerard. Course fit layer EMPTY (all 72 players NO_HISTORY despite Memorial being annual since 1976 — Phase 1 course history pull failed). BBI beat DG (+0.033).
events.course_fit_alert: ALL 72 players NO_HISTORY — Apollo Phase 1 course history lookup failed for event_id=23. Rich history expected. Investigate tpc_sawgrass_history.json and Apollo logs.

events.course: TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley (North Course)
events.notes: Cauley wins at -27 from BBI #21. BBI LOST to DG (-0.046 delta). Burns (#1 BBI) T21 — courseFit over-inflated by multi-venue RBC history (only 2025 data = TPC Toronto North Course). Fitzpatrick (#3 BBI) T2 ✓. Hovland (#9 BBI) T3 ✓. Baseline v8.5 beat all 5 shadow variants. Spearman not statistically significant (p=0.094). Improvement priority: venue-year filtering for RBC Canadian Open course history.
events.course_history_note: 53.5% coverage (77/144) but only 2025 data is TPC Toronto North Course. Years 2022-2024 were at St. George's, Oakdale, Hamilton — different venues. True course fit coverage ~18-25%.

history.notes: manual catch-up run — scheduled Mnemosyne stalled Mon Apr 20
Zurich Classic team format — paused_team_event mode
All 70 players made cut (small field 72). Spearman 0.303 (p=0.011, significant). Top-10 overlap 4/10. Cameron Young BBI#2 wins.

Kristoffer Reitan wins from BBI rank #43 (DG rank #36). McIlroy (#1 BBI) T22; Schauffele (#2 BBI) T60. Both models struggled with low-ranked surprise winner. BBI beat DG narrowly (+0.032). Small field (72 players, no missed cuts in DataGolf feed).
BBI 0.4309 — above 0.40 target. Aaron Rai wins (BBI #63, surprise). Rahm T2 (BBI #3) ✓, McIlroy T7 (BBI #2) ✓, Aberg T4 (BBI #8) ✓, Schauffele T7 (BBI #5) ✓. DG preds unavailable post-event.

history.skip_reason: pipeline_not_run — CURRENT_EVENT.json was set to Charles Schwab Challenge on 2026-05-21 before CJ Cup Byron Nelson began. No BBI file generated.
history.notes: Pipeline miss: Mnemosyne (2026-05-21) auto-detected CJ Cup Byron Nelson as 'upcoming' but set CURRENT_EVENT.json to Charles Schwab Challenge. Apollo/Phase1 never ran for event_id=19. Wyndham Clark won at -30.
Backfilled 2026-06-08. Pipeline ran week of May 27; postmortem skipped in prior Mnemosyne run. Henley wins at -20.

history.event: the Memorial Tournament presented by Workday
history.notes: Poston wins playoff at -12. Course fit layer absent (all NO_HISTORY). BBI beat DG +0.033.

Cauley wins at -27 (BBI #21). DG beat BBI (+0.046 DG advantage). Burns (#1 BBI) T21, Fleetwood (#2 BBI) T13. Fitzpatrick (#3) T2 ✓, Hovland (#9) T3 ✓. Baseline v8.5 beat all shadow variants. RBC course history coverage inflated by multi-venue data — only 2025 = TPC Toronto North.

improvement_trend.direction: stable (Masters dip from Masters-specific field challenge)

dg_head_to_head.description: Week-by-week comparison of BBI Spearman vs DataGolf pre-tournament ranking Spearman.
dg_head_to_head.note: Through week 19. Season record: BBI 6W-3L vs DG.

field_size_reference.note: When comparing Spearman across events with different field sizes, use z-scores.
